title_hint: Story2Proposal-Style Structured Writing with a Graph Runtime
topic: structured scientific paper generation with graph-based agents
problem_statement: Large language models can draft papers, but unconstrained multi-agent writing often drifts across sections, loses evidence alignment, and mishandles figures and citations.
motivation: Researchers need a scaffold that writes papers in a structured way while preserving explicit section-level obligations and revision loops.
core_idea: Use a graph-driven agent runtime with a shared manuscript contract so that architect, writer, evaluators, refiner, and renderer coordinate through explicit section constraints instead of free-form generation.
method_summary: The system starts from a structured research story, builds a manuscript blueprint, initializes a persistent contract, writes sections sequentially, evaluates each section along reasoning, structure, and visual dimensions, applies contract-aware revisions, and finally renders markdown and LaTeX outputs.
contributions: A contract-centered pipeline for structured scientific writing
A graph runtime that supports explicit routing and section-level revision loops
A reproducible scaffold that preserves figure and citation obligations during drafting

experiments.name: Claim-evidence alignment study
experiments.setup: Compare unconstrained long-form generation against contract-centered drafting on the same set of structured stories.
experiments.result_summary: The contract-centered pipeline improves section coverage and reduces unsupported claims.

experiments.name: Revision loop ablation
experiments.setup: Remove evaluator-driven rewrite cycles and measure structural consistency regressions.
experiments.result_summary: Evaluator-driven rewrite cycles reduce downstream rendering warnings and improve consistency.

baselines: multi-agent writing without a persistent contract
findings: Persistent structured contracts make section outputs easier to validate and refine.
Sequential section writing is more stable than fully parallel drafting in the MVP setting.
Explicit visual placeholders reduce figure-reference mismatches during rendering.
limitations: The MVP uses placeholder figures instead of true figure synthesis.
Reference handling is based on seeded bibliography entries rather than live retrieval.

references.title: Story2Proposal: A Scaffold for Structured Scientific Paper Writing
Graph-Oriented Agent Execution for Tool-Integrated Workflows
